basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Al-Aḥqāf — “The Sand-Dunes” — is a Meccan sūra that opens with the letters Ḥā-Mīm and the reminder that the heavens and the earth were created only in truth and for a named term. It confronts the denial of resurrection and the futility of idols, then rises toward filial tenderness: the verse of the fortieth year, where man, come to his maturity, implores to be inspired to gratitude toward God and his parents. Qushayrī makes of it the school of all deference — whoever does not honor the dignity of his own kind is yet further from rightly observing that of his Master — and recalls the prophetic word: “The Lord's good pleasure lies in the parents' good pleasure.” Then comes the account of Hūd and the ʿĀd, annihilated in the dunes by the wind they took for a rain-cloud, and the company of jinn who listen to the Qurʾān, observe the courtesy of silence, and believe. The sūra is sealed upon the call to the patience of the messengers “of firm resolve” (ūlū al-ʿazm), Qushayrī defining patience as standing by the decree of God without complaint or constraint, and the duration of the world as a single glance set against the beginningless eternity.
themes: creation in truth and for a term
Hūd, the ʿĀd, and the Sand-Dunes
the jinn who listen to the Qurʾān
the patience of the resolute prophets

versets.ar: حم
versets.tr: ḥā mīm
versets.trad: Ḥā. Mīm.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the hearts of the people of His care He has shielded; He turned away from them the promptings of doubt and steadied them in the contemplations of certainty by the light of realization, so that the proofs shone within them. To this He joined the subtleties of His kindness, and nourished them with the breeze of intimacy in the courts of nearness. The two letters open the sūra upon the secret of the revealed Word.

mots.def: opening letters whose meaning belongs to the divine secret.

versets.ar: تَنْزِيلُ الْكِتَابِ مِنَ اللَّهِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَكِيمِ
versets.tr: tanzīlu l-kitābi mina llāhi l-ʿazīzi l-ḥakīmi
versets.trad: The sending down of the Book is from God, the Mighty, the Wise.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī ties the two Names to the event of Revelation: al-ʿAzīz, the One who gives might to the believers by sending down upon them the Book; al-Ḥakīm, the One who has made His Book inviolable, secure from all alteration and change. The descent of the Qurʾān is thus an act of both power and wisdom.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / ك ت ب
mots.sens: the sending down of the Book

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ح ك م

versets.ar: مَا خَلَقْنَا السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا إِلَّا بِالْحَقِّ وَأَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى ۚ وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا عَمَّا أُنذِرُوا مُعْرِضُونَ
versets.tr: mā khalaqnā s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa wa-mā baynahumā illā bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-ajalin musamman wa-lladhīna kafarū ʿammā unḏirū muʿriḍūna
versets.trad: We created the heavens and the earth and what lies between them only in truth and for an appointed term. Yet those who disbelieve turn away from what they are warned of.
versets.commentaire: All creation rests upon the Real (al-ḥaqq) and is assigned only a named term: nothing here is gratuitous or everlasting. Qushayrī observes that the disbelievers turn away from the very site of the warning and persist in their obstinacy: warned, they turn their backs not from ignorance but by deliberate refusal.

mots.def: al-ḥaqq: the Real, the true, the just.

mots.racine: أ ج ل / س م و

versets.ar: قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُم مَّا تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ أَرُونِي مَاذَا خَلَقُوا مِنَ الْأَرْضِ أَمْ لَهُمْ شِرْكٌ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ ۖ ائْتُونِي بِكِتَابٍ مِّن قَبْلِ هَٰذَا أَوْ أَثَارَةٍ مِّنْ عِلْمٍ إِن كُنتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: qul a-raʾaytum mā tadʿūna min dūni llāhi arūnī māḏā khalaqū mina l-arḍi am lahum shirkun fī s-samāwāti iʾtūnī bi-kitābin min qabli hāḏā aw aṯāratin min ʿilmin in kuntum ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: Say: “Consider those you call upon besides God; show me what they have created of the earth, or whether they have any share in the heavens! Bring me a Book older than this one, or some vestige of knowledge, if you are truthful.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī renders the challenge as a summons: show me what trace your idols leave in the kingdom, what power they have to harm or to benefit! If you hold an argument, produce it; an indication, set it forth. But since you are unable, why do you not turn back from your error and renounce it? The very absence of proof is itself the proof.

mots.racine: د ع و / د و ن
mots.sens: what you call upon besides God

mots.racine: أ ث ر / ع ل م

versets.ar: وَمَنْ أَضَلُّ مِمَّن يَدْعُو مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ مَن لَّا يَسْتَجِيبُ لَهُ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ وَهُمْ عَن دُعَائِهِمْ غَافِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-man aḍallu mimman yadʿū min dūni llāhi man lā yastajību lahu ilā yawmi l-qiyāmati wa-hum ʿan duʿāʾihim ghāfilūna
versets.trad: Who is more astray than one who calls, besides God, upon those who will not answer him till the Day of Resurrection, and who are heedless of their call,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī puts the obvious as rhetorical question: who is more deeply astray than one who worships an inert thing, lifeless, incapable of the least benefit or harm? To address one's prayer to what neither hears nor answers is to consign oneself to a final silence.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا حُشِرَ النَّاسُ كَانُوا لَهُمْ أَعْدَاءً وَكَانُوا بِعِبَادَتِهِمْ كَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-iḏā ḥushira n-nāsu kānū lahum aʿdāʾan wa-kānū bi-ʿibādatihim kāfirīna
versets.trad: and who, when mankind is gathered, will be their enemies and will reject their worship?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sums up the eschatological scene: when mankind is gathered for the Reckoning, enmity breaks out between the idols and those who worshipped them. What one has deified turns against the worshipper and disowns his cult: the object of idolatry becomes the accuser.

mots.def: kāfir: here, one who disowns, denies.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتُنَا بَيِّنَاتٍ قَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَاءَهُمْ هَٰذَا سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: wa-iḏā tutlā ʿalayhim āyātunā bayyinātin qāla lladhīna kafarū li-l-ḥaqqi lammā jāʾahum hāḏā siḥrun mubīnun
versets.trad: When Our signs are recited to them as clear proofs, those who disbelieve say of the Truth when it comes to them: “This is plain magic!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes the chain of accusations: they charged Our messengers with sorcery, then with forgery and trickery. When the Real reaches them as dazzling signs, they recast it as a spell, preferring insult to acknowledgment.

mots.racine: ت ل و / أ ي ي
mots.sens: Our signs are recited to them
mots.def: talā: to recite; āyāt: signs, verses.

mots.racine: س ح ر / ب ي ن

versets.ar: أَمْ يَقُولُونَ افْتَرَاهُ ۖ قُلْ إِنِ افْتَرَيْتُهُ فَلَا تَمْلِكُونَ لِي مِنَ اللَّهِ شَيْئًا ۖ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا تُفِيضُونَ فِيهِ ۖ كَفَىٰ بِهِ شَهِيدًا بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ ۖ وَهُوَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ
versets.tr: am yaqūlūna ftarāhu qul ini ftaraytuhu fa-lā tamlikūna lī mina llāhi shayʾan huwa aʿlamu bi-mā tufīḍūna fīhi kafā bihi shahīdan baynī wa-baynakum wa-huwa l-ghafūru r-raḥīmu
versets.trad: Or do they say: “He has forged it”? Say: “If I have forged it, you can avail me nothing against God. He knows best what you pour forth concerning it. He suffices as Witness between me and you, and He is the Forgiving, the Compassionate.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī places upon the Prophet's lips a word of pure sincerity: “Let God suffice as Witness between me and you: you have set up partners beside Him; I have devoted to Him a pure monotheism.” Recourse to the divine Testimony suffices where human argument is exhausted, and the verse closes upon the Names of forgiveness.

mots.def: afāḍa fī: to pour into speech.

mots.racine: ك ف ي / ش ه د

versets.ar: قُلْ مَا كُنتُ بِدْعًا مِّنَ الرُّسُلِ وَمَا أَدْرِي مَا يُفْعَلُ بِي وَلَا بِكُمْ ۖ إِنْ أَتَّبِعُ إِلَّا مَا يُوحَىٰ إِلَيَّ وَمَا أَنَا إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: qul mā kuntu bidʿan mina r-rusuli wa-mā adrī mā yufʿalu bī wa-lā bikum in attabiʿu illā mā yūḥā ilayya wa-mā anā illā naḏīrun mubīnun
versets.trad: Say: “I am no innovation among the messengers, and I do not know what will be done with me or with you. I follow only what is revealed to me, and I am only a clear warner.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: I am not the first messenger sent, nor have I come with anything other than the foundations of monotheism they brought; I have commanded you only sincerity in Unicity, truthfulness in servitude, and the call to noble virtues. And the “I do not know what will be done with me” teaches that the matter is His matter, the decree His decree: He disposes of His servants as He wills — a confession of pure dependence.

mots.racine: ب د ع / ر س ل
ن ذ ر / ب ي ن

versets.ar: قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ إِن كَانَ مِنْ عِندِ اللَّهِ وَكَفَرْتُم بِهِ وَشَهِدَ شَاهِدٌ مِّن بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ عَلَىٰ مِثْلِهِ فَآمَنَ وَاسْتَكْبَرْتُمْ ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: qul a-raʾaytum in kāna min ʿindi llāhi wa-kafartum bihi wa-shahida shāhidun min banī isrāʾīla ʿalā miṯlihi fa-āmana wa-stakbartum inna llāha lā yahdī l-qawma ẓ-ẓālimīna
versets.trad: Say: “Consider: if this is from God and you disbelieve in it, while a witness from the Children of Israel testifies to its like, and believes, while you wax proud—? God does not guide the wrongdoing people.”
versets.commentaire: The witness is, according to the majority, ʿAbd Allāh ibn Salām. Qushayrī concludes: it is made plain to him that they have, in no case, any excuse, nor any safety from God's punishment; all the arguments with which they lull themselves are, in truth, void. God announced the coming of Muḥammad in every earlier Book, but the Prophet's contemporaries concealed it out of jealousy.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَوْ كَانَ خَيْرًا مَّا سَبَقُونَا إِلَيْهِ ۚ وَإِذْ لَمْ يَهْتَدُوا بِهِ فَسَيَقُولُونَ هَٰذَا إِفْكٌ قَدِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhīna kafarū li-lladhīna āmanū law kāna khayran mā sabaqūnā ilayhi wa-iḏ lam yahtadū bihi fa-sayaqūlūna hāḏā ifkun qadīmun
versets.trad: Those who disbelieve say of those who believe: “Had it been any good, they would not have outstripped us to it.” And since they are not guided by it, they will say: “This is an ancient lie!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī dismantles their pride: they claim that, were this message true, their rank with God would be inferior to none, and they would have outstripped all others in merit. Finding no argument for this claim, they fall back on insult: “an ancient lie.” Their scorn for the first converts betrays the fear of being surpassed.

mots.sens: they would not have outstripped us

mots.racine: أ ف ك / ق د م

versets.ar: وَمِن قَبْلِهِ كِتَابُ مُوسَىٰ إِمَامًا وَرَحْمَةً ۚ وَهَٰذَا كِتَابٌ مُّصَدِّقٌ لِّسَانًا عَرَبِيًّا لِّيُنذِرَ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا وَبُشْرَىٰ لِلْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-min qablihi kitābu mūsā imāman wa-raḥmatan wa-hāḏā kitābun muṣaddiqun lisānan ʿarabiyyan li-yunḏira lladhīna ẓalamū wa-bushrā li-l-muḥsinīna
versets.trad: Before it was the Book of Moses, a guide and a mercy. And this is a Book confirming it, in the Arabic tongue, to warn the wrongdoers and as good tidings for those who do good.
versets.commentaire: The Qurʾān confirms (muṣaddiq) the Torah, guide and mercy before it: the unity of the message runs through the Revelations. Qushayrī underscores the Book's double function, a warning for the unjust and good tidings for the doers of good (muḥsinūn): one and the same Word becomes dread or joy according to the heart that receives it.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / أ م م
mots.sens: the Book of Moses, a guide

mots.racine: ب ش ر / ح س ن
mots.sens: good tidings for the doers of good

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قَالُوا رَبُّنَا اللَّهُ ثُمَّ اسْتَقَامُوا فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna qālū rabbunā llāhu ṯumma staqāmū fa-lā khawfun ʿalayhim wa-lā hum yaḥzanūna
versets.trad: Those who say: “Our Lord is God,” and then hold straight—no fear shall be upon them, nor shall they grieve.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever rises upon faith and rectitude attains every dignity and reaches abundant safety. He notes a subtlety of language: the sīn of “istiqāma” is a sīn of seeking; the upright one is he who implores God to establish him upon the Real and to steady him in truthfulness. Uprightness is not a possession but a grace ceaselessly sought.

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ الْجَنَّةِ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا جَزَاءً بِمَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: ulāʾika aṣḥābu l-jannati khālidīna fīhā jazāʾan bimā kānū yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: Those are the companions of the Garden, abiding therein forever, a recompense for what they used to do.
versets.commentaire: Uprightness here bears its fruit: lasting abode in the Garden. Following Qushayrī, the requital is no wrenched wage but the very unfolding of the steadfast servant's state: the abundant safety promised to whoever held straight becomes an eternity of nearness.

mots.racine: ص ح ب / ج ن ن

versets.ar: وَوَصَّيْنَا الْإِنسَانَ بِوَالِدَيْهِ إِحْسَانًا ۖ حَمَلَتْهُ أُمُّهُ كُرْهًا وَوَضَعَتْهُ كُرْهًا ۖ وَحَمْلُهُ وَفِصَالُهُ ثَلَاثُونَ شَهْرًا ۚ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا بَلَغَ أَشُدَّهُ وَبَلَغَ أَرْبَعِينَ سَنَةً قَالَ رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِي أَنْ أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيَّ وَعَلَىٰ وَالِدَيَّ وَأَنْ أَعْمَلَ صَالِحًا تَرْضَاهُ وَأَصْلِحْ لِي فِي ذُرِّيَّتِي ۖ إِنِّي تُبْتُ إِلَيْكَ وَإِنِّي مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-waṣṣaynā l-insāna bi-wālidayhi iḥsānan ḥamalathu ummuhu kurhan wa-waḍaʿathu kurhan wa-ḥamluhu wa-fiṣāluhu ṯalāṯūna shahran ḥattā iḏā balagha ashuddahu wa-balagha arbaʿīna sanatan qāla rabbi awziʿnī an ashkura niʿmataka llatī anʿamta ʿalayya wa-ʿalā wālidayya wa-an aʿmala ṣāliḥan tarḍāhu wa-aṣliḥ lī fī ḏurriyyatī innī tubtu ilayka wa-innī mina l-muslimīna
versets.trad: We have enjoined upon man kindness to his parents. His mother bore him in pain and gave birth to him in pain; his bearing and his weaning are thirty months. Until, when he reaches his full strength and reaches forty years, he says: “My Lord, inspire me to give thanks for Your favor which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents, and to do righteousness that pleases You; and make righteous for me my offspring. I have turned to You, and I am among those who submit (muslimūn).”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes filial respect the school of all deference: man is commanded to honor his parents' right out of regard for the favor of upbringing and nourishment; whoever does not honor the dignity of his own kind is yet further from rightly observing that of his Master. He recalls the prophetic word: “The Lord's good pleasure lies in the parents' good pleasure, and His wrath in their wrath.” And he distinguishes the two regards: to the father the right of respect, to the mother that of tenderness and reverence.

mots.racine: و ص ي / أ ن س
و ل د / ح س ن

mots.def: awzaʿa: to dispose the heart, inspire.

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ نَتَقَبَّلُ عَنْهُمْ أَحْسَنَ مَا عَمِلُوا وَنَتَجَاوَزُ عَن سَيِّئَاتِهِمْ فِي أَصْحَابِ الْجَنَّةِ ۖ وَعْدَ الصِّدْقِ الَّذِي كَانُوا يُوعَدُونَ
versets.tr: ulāʾika lladhīna nataqabbalu ʿanhum aḥsana mā ʿamilū wa-natajāwazu ʿan sayyiʾātihim fī aṣḥābi l-jannati waʿda ṣ-ṣidqi lladhī kānū yūʿadūna
versets.trad: Those are they from whom We accept the best of what they did, and We overlook their misdeeds—among the companions of the Garden. The truthful promise that they were promised!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī links this acceptance to filial piety: the acceptance of obedience and the forgiveness of fault are conditioned upon kindness to parents. He warns, conversely: God blamed the one who shows them even a sigh of irritation—a hint of what worse would follow in harshness—and whoever yields to it belongs to the people of loss, deficient in his faith. Thus the servant's way is first to mend what binds him to God, and his bond with creatures then sets itself right.

mots.racine: ج و ز / س و أ
mots.def: tajāwaza: to pardon, not hold against.

mots.racine: و ع د / ص د ق

versets.ar: وَالَّذِي قَالَ لِوَالِدَيْهِ أُفٍّ لَّكُمَا أَتَعِدَانِنِي أَنْ أُخْرَجَ وَقَدْ خَلَتِ الْقُرُونُ مِن قَبْلِي وَهُمَا يَسْتَغِيثَانِ اللَّهَ وَيْلَكَ آمِنْ إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقٌّ فَيَقُولُ مَا هَٰذَا إِلَّا أَسَاطِيرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhī qāla li-wālidayhi uffin lakumā a-taʿidāninī an ukhraja wa-qad khalati l-qurūnu min qablī wa-humā yastaghīṯāni llāha waylaka āmin inna waʿda llāhi ḥaqqun fa-yaqūlu mā hāḏā illā asāṭīru l-awwalīna
versets.trad: But there is one who says to his parents: “Fie upon you both! Do you promise me that I shall be brought forth, when generations have passed away before me?” And they cry to God for help: “Woe to you! Believe! The promise of God is true.” And he replies: “These are nothing but the legends of the ancients.”
versets.commentaire: Opposite the grateful son stands the ingrate. Qushayrī recalls that God blamed the one who flings at his parents the “uff” of irritation, and that the worst trait of a child is to chafe at his parents' long life and to be pained by having to honor their right. He warns gravely: soon the origin dies and the lineage remains; yet the lineage necessarily follows the origin—he who mistreats will be treated likewise by his own.

mots.racine: و ع د / خ ر ج
mots.sens: do you promise me to be raised
mots.def: waʿada: to promise; ukhraj: to be brought forth.

mots.racine: س ط ر / أ و ل

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ حَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الْقَوْلُ فِي أُمَمٍ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّنَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنسِ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا خَاسِرِينَ
versets.tr: ulāʾika lladhīna ḥaqqa ʿalayhimu l-qawlu fī umamin qad khalat min qablihim mina l-jinni wa-l-insi innahum kānū khāsirīna
versets.trad: Those are they against whom the Word has been realized, among nations of jinn and men that passed away before them. Truly they were the losers.
versets.commentaire: The decree is realized against the deniers as it was against the effaced nations of jinn and men. In Qushayrī's perspective their loss (khusrān) is no external accident but the very fruit of their refusal: having turned their backs on the site of the warning, they inherit only the void they chose.

mots.racine: ح ق ق / ق و ل
mots.sens: the Word is realized against them

mots.racine: أ م م / خ ل و

versets.ar: وَلِكُلٍّ دَرَجَاتٌ مِّمَّا عَمِلُوا ۖ وَلِيُوَفِّيَهُمْ أَعْمَالَهُمْ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-li-kullin darajātun mimmā ʿamilū wa-li-yuwaffiyahum aʿmālahum wa-hum lā yuẓlamūna
versets.trad: For each are degrees according to what they did, that He may pay them in full for their deeds; and they shall not be wronged.
versets.commentaire: To each his degrees according to his acts: the hierarchy of states reflects exactly the soul's labor. In Qushayrī's spirit, the full and unwronging requital shows that divine justice is also mercy, for none loses there the least particle of his effort—every step toward the Real is reckoned.

mots.racine: و ف ي / ع م ل
mots.sens: to pay them in full for their works

versets.ar: وَيَوْمَ يُعْرَضُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا عَلَى النَّارِ أَذْهَبْتُمْ طَيِّبَاتِكُمْ فِي حَيَاتِكُمُ الدُّنْيَا وَاسْتَمْتَعْتُم بِهَا فَالْيَوْمَ تُجْزَوْنَ عَذَابَ الْهُونِ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَسْتَكْبِرُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِّ وَبِمَا كُنتُمْ تَفْسُقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yawma yuʿraḍu lladhīna kafarū ʿalā n-nāri aḏhabtum ṭayyibātikum fī ḥayātikumu d-dunyā wa-stamtaʿtum bihā fa-l-yawma tujzawna ʿaḏāba l-hūni bimā kuntum tastakbirūna fī l-arḍi bi-ghayri l-ḥaqqi wa-bimā kuntum tafsuqūna
versets.trad: On the Day when those who disbelieved are exposed to the Fire, it will be said: “You squandered your good things in your worldly life and took your enjoyment of them. So today you are recompensed with the chastisement of humiliation, because you were unjustly arrogant on the earth, and because you were transgressors.”
versets.commentaire: From this verse Qushayrī draws a rule for the wayfarer: the servant's way is never to forget his Adored in any state, to remember that He is with him in his sorrow and his joy, in his intimate discourse in ease as in trial. If intimacy comes to him and hope, then expansion, prevail, then a contraction descends or fear touches him, let him address his Lord—so as not to be of those told: “You squandered your good things in your worldly life.”

mots.racine: ع ر ض / ن و ر
mots.sens: they are exposed to the Fire

mots.racine: ذ ه ب / ط ي ب
ع ذ ب / ه و ن

versets.ar: وَاذْكُرْ أَخَا عَادٍ إِذْ أَنذَرَ قَوْمَهُ بِالْأَحْقَافِ وَقَدْ خَلَتِ النُّذُرُ مِن بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِ أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوا إِلَّا اللَّهَ إِنِّي أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ
versets.tr: wa-ḏkur akhā ʿādin iḏ anḏara qawmahu bi-l-aḥqāfi wa-qad khalati n-nuḏuru min bayni yadayhi wa-min khalfihi allā taʿbudū illā llāha innī akhāfu ʿalaykum ʿaḏāba yawmin ʿaẓīmin
versets.trad: And recall the brother of ʿĀd, when he warned his people in the Sand-Dunes (al-Aḥqāf)—and warners had passed away before him and after him—: “Worship none but God! I fear for you the chastisement of a tremendous Day.”
versets.commentaire: The “brother of ʿĀd” is Hūd. Qushayrī summarizes: God sets forth in detail the story of Hūd and his people ʿĀd, the discourse that passed between them, the reproach that fell upon them, and how He seized them with a painful chastisement. The aḥqāf are, he specifies, great dunes of sand, curved and winding, that do not reach the size of mountains—the desolate setting of a people deaf to warning.

mots.racine: أ خ و / ع و د
mots.def: akh: brother; here Hūd, of the tribe of ʿĀd.
aḥqāf, pl. of ḥiqf: long curved sand-dunes.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ع ظ م
mots.sens: the chastisement of a tremendous Day

versets.ar: قَالُوا أَجِئْتَنَا لِتَأْفِكَنَا عَنْ آلِهَتِنَا فَأْتِنَا بِمَا تَعِدُنَا إِن كُنتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ
versets.tr: qālū a-jiʾtanā li-taʾfikanā ʿan ālihatinā fa-ʾtinā bimā taʿidunā in kunta mina ṣ-ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: They said: “Have you come to us to turn us away from our gods? Then bring upon us what you threaten us with, if you are truthful!”
versets.commentaire: The ʿĀd answer the warning with provocation: they dare Hūd to hasten the chastisement rather than relent. In Qushayrī's reading, this rush to demand punishment betrays the blindness of a heart clinging to its idols, taking the divine forbearance for impotence.

versets.ar: قَالَ إِنَّمَا الْعِلْمُ عِندَ اللَّهِ وَأُبَلِّغُكُم مَّا أُرْسِلْتُ بِهِ وَلَٰكِنِّي أَرَاكُمْ قَوْمًا تَجْهَلُونَ
versets.tr: qāla innamā l-ʿilmu ʿinda llāhi wa-uballighukum mā ursiltu bihi wa-lākinnī arākum qawman tajhalūna
versets.trad: He said: “Knowledge is only with God. I convey to you what I was sent with; but I see you are a people in ignorance.”
versets.commentaire: Hūd refers the knowledge of the time and form of the chastisement to God alone: his charge is conveyance, not mastery of the unseen. In Qushayrī's spirit, this is the servant's right posture—to render knowledge to its Owner and to recognize that the ʿĀd's ignorance is no lack of information but a refusal to see.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا رَأَوْهُ عَارِضًا مُّسْتَقْبِلَ أَوْدِيَتِهِمْ قَالُوا هَٰذَا عَارِضٌ مُّمْطِرُنَا ۚ بَلْ هُوَ مَا اسْتَعْجَلْتُم بِهِ ۖ رِيحٌ فِيهَا عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: fa-lammā raʾawhu ʿāriḍan mustaqbila awdiyatihim qālū hāḏā ʿāriḍun mumṭirunā bal huwa mā staʿjaltum bihi rīḥun fīhā ʿaḏābun alīmun
versets.trad: Then when they saw it as a cloud advancing toward their valleys, they said: “This is a cloud that will bring us rain!” Nay, it is what you sought to hasten: a wind bearing a painful chastisement,
versets.commentaire: They take for beneficent rain what is their ruin: the awaited cloud is the wind of the chastisement they themselves demanded. Following Qushayrī, it is the image of the blind heart that hails as grace what destroys it, discerning the meaning of the signs only when it is too late.

mots.def: ʿāriḍ: a cloud spanning the sky.

mots.racine: ر و ح / ع ذ ب / أ ل م
mots.sens: a wind bearing a painful chastisement

versets.ar: تُدَمِّرُ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ بِأَمْرِ رَبِّهَا فَأَصْبَحُوا لَا يُرَىٰ إِلَّا مَسَاكِنُهُمْ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِي الْقَوْمَ الْمُجْرِمِينَ
versets.tr: tudammiru kulla shayʾin bi-amri rabbihā fa-aṣbaḥū lā yurā illā masākinuhum kaḏālika najzī l-qawma l-mujrimīna
versets.trad: destroying everything by the command of its Lord. By morning nothing was to be seen but their dwellings. Thus do We recompense the criminal people.
versets.commentaire: The wind destroys all “by the command of its Lord”: it is creation's very obedience that executes the decree. Qushayrī, meditating on ʿĀd, concludes soberly: all We had granted them availed them nothing… consider, then, how We annihilated them. Of the mighty people nothing remains but empty dwellings.

mots.racine: أ م ر / ر ب ب
mots.sens: by the command of its Lord

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ مَكَّنَّاهُمْ فِيمَا إِن مَّكَّنَّاكُمْ فِيهِ وَجَعَلْنَا لَهُمْ سَمْعًا وَأَبْصَارًا وَأَفْئِدَةً فَمَا أَغْنَىٰ عَنْهُمْ سَمْعُهُمْ وَلَا أَبْصَارُهُمْ وَلَا أَفْئِدَتُهُم مِّن شَيْءٍ إِذْ كَانُوا يَجْحَدُونَ بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ وَحَاقَ بِهِم مَّا كَانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِئُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad makkannāhum fīmā in makkannākum fīhi wa-jaʿalnā lahum samʿan wa-abṣāran wa-afʾidatan fa-mā aghnā ʿanhum samʿuhum wa-lā abṣāruhum wa-lā afʾidatuhum min shayʾin iḏ kānū yajḥadūna bi-āyāti llāhi wa-ḥāqa bihim mā kānū bihi yastahziʾūna
versets.trad: We had established them in that wherein We have not established you, and We gave them hearing, sight, and hearts. Yet their hearing, their sight, and their hearts availed them nothing when they denied the signs of God; and that which they mocked closed in upon them.
versets.commentaire: Showered with strength, hearing, sight, and heart, the ʿĀd drew nothing from them once they denied the signs. Qushayrī comments soberly: what We had given them availed them nothing. The organs of perception are worth only the heart that consents; refused to the Real, they become a sterile gift, and the mockery closes in upon the mocker.

mots.racine: س م ع / ب ص ر / ف أ د
mots.def: afʾida, pl. of fuʾād: hearts, seats of intellect.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ أَهْلَكْنَا مَا حَوْلَكُم مِّنَ الْقُرَىٰ وَصَرَّفْنَا الْآيَاتِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad ahlaknā mā ḥawlakum mina l-qurā wa-ṣarrafnā l-āyāti laʿallahum yarjiʿūna
versets.trad: We have destroyed the towns around you, and We varied the signs, that they might return.
versets.commentaire: God recalls the neighboring towns destroyed and the signs variously deployed “that they might return.” In Qushayrī's perspective, the variation of signs is mercy: God multiplies the calls and warnings to open the heart a thousand doors of return, so that ruin befalls only at the price of obstinate refusal.

mots.racine: ه ل ك / ح و ل
mots.sens: We destroyed what is around you

mots.def: qurā, pl. of qarya: villages, towns.

mots.racine: ص ر ف / أ ي ي
mots.def: ṣarrafa: to diversify, repeat in varied forms.

versets.ar: فَلَوْلَا نَصَرَهُمُ الَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ قُرْبَانًا آلِهَةً ۖ بَلْ ضَلُّوا عَنْهُمْ ۚ وَذَٰلِكَ إِفْكُهُمْ وَمَا كَانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ
versets.tr: fa-law-lā naṣarahumu lladhīna ttakhaḏū min dūni llāhi qurbānan ālihatan bal ḍallū ʿanhum wa-ḏālika ifkuhum wa-mā kānū yaftarūna
versets.trad: Why then did those they had taken besides God as gods for nearness not help them? Nay, they strayed from them. Such was their lie and what they used to fabricate.
versets.commentaire: On the day of trial, the false gods taken as a means of nearness and intercession “strayed from them”: they vanish instead of helping. In Qushayrī's spirit, this desertion is the very refutation of the lie (ifk): what one has fabricated as a support proves empty at the very moment of greatest need.

mots.sens: why did they not help them

mots.racine: ق ر ب / أ ل ه
mots.sens: gods as a means of nearness
mots.def: qurbān: a means of drawing near.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ صَرَفْنَا إِلَيْكَ نَفَرًا مِّنَ الْجِنِّ يَسْتَمِعُونَ الْقُرْآنَ فَلَمَّا حَضَرُوهُ قَالُوا أَنصِتُوا ۖ فَلَمَّا قُضِيَ وَلَّوْا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِم مُّنذِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-iḏ ṣarafnā ilayka nafaran mina l-jinni yastamiʿūna l-qurʾāna fa-lammā ḥaḍarūhu qālū anṣitū fa-lammā quḍiya wallaw ilā qawmihim munḏirīna
versets.trad: And recall when We turned toward you a company of jinn to listen to the Qurʾān; and when they were present at it they said: “Be silent and listen!” And when it was concluded they returned to their people as warners.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī recalls that the Messenger was sent to the jinn as to mankind: this group came, believed, and returned to warn their own, so that among the jinn today there are believers and deniers. And from their “Be silent!” he draws a lesson of adab: when the jinn were present at the carpet of His service, they enjoined upon one another good comportment; for the people of presence are marked by self-effacement, stillness, awe, and gravity, while agitation betrays absence and a lack of vigilance.

mots.racine: ص ر ف / ن ف ر
mots.sens: We turned toward you a company
mots.def: nafar: a small band of men or jinn.

mots.sens: they said: be silent and listen

mots.racine: و ل ي / ن ذ ر
mots.def: wallā: to turn toward; munḏir: warner.

versets.ar: قَالُوا يَا قَوْمَنَا إِنَّا سَمِعْنَا كِتَابًا أُنزِلَ مِن بَعْدِ مُوسَىٰ مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ يَهْدِي إِلَى الْحَقِّ وَإِلَىٰ طَرِيقٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ
versets.tr: qālū yā qawmanā innā samiʿnā kitāban unzila min baʿdi mūsā muṣaddiqan limā bayna yadayhi yahdī ilā l-ḥaqqi wa-ilā ṭarīqin mustaqīmin
versets.trad: They said: “O our people! We have heard a Book sent down after Moses, confirming what came before it, guiding to the Truth and to a straight path.
versets.commentaire: The jinn report to their people a Book confirming the Torah and guiding “to the Real and to a straight path.” Following Qushayrī, their warner's zeal illustrates the natural fruit of true listening: whoever has tasted the Word in the silence of the heart cannot but become, in turn, a summons toward the Real.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ن ز ل
mots.sens: a Book sent down after Moses

mots.racine: ط ر ق / ق و م

versets.ar: يَا قَوْمَنَا أَجِيبُوا دَاعِيَ اللَّهِ وَآمِنُوا بِهِ يَغْفِرْ لَكُم مِّن ذُنُوبِكُمْ وَيُجِرْكُم مِّنْ عَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ
versets.tr: yā qawmanā ajībū dāʿiya llāhi wa-āminū bihi yaghfir lakum min ḏunūbikum wa-yujirkum min ʿaḏābin alīmin
versets.trad: O our people! Answer the caller of God and believe in him! He will forgive you some of your sins and protect you from a painful chastisement.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds here a subtlety on answering the call: responding is of two kinds: responding to God and responding to the one who calls. The response to the caller comes through contemplating the intermediary—the Messenger; the response to God comes aloud when the message arrives by the envoy's tongue, and in secret when the warnings come to the heart. Thus there is one who responds by his soul, one by his heart, one by his spirit, and one by his innermost secret. And whoever delays in answering is deprived of what was addressed to him.

mots.racine: ج و ب / د ع و

versets.ar: وَمَن لَّا يُجِبْ دَاعِيَ اللَّهِ فَلَيْسَ بِمُعْجِزٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَيْسَ لَهُ مِن دُونِهِ أَوْلِيَاءُ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ فِي ضَلَالٍ مُّبِينٍ
versets.tr: wa-man lā yujib dāʿiya llāhi fa-laysa bi-muʿjizin fī l-arḍi wa-laysa lahu min dūnihi awliyāʾu ulāʾika fī ḍalālin mubīnin
versets.trad: Whoever does not answer the caller of God cannot frustrate Him on the earth, nor has he any protectors besides Him. Such are in manifest error.
versets.commentaire: Whoever refuses the call can neither thwart God nor find protectors besides Him. In Qushayrī's reading, the “manifest error” is the obverse of the preceding verse: having failed to respond by soul, heart, spirit, or secret, the recalcitrant finds himself without support, given over to the void his own refusal has hollowed out.

mots.def: muʿjiz: one who renders powerless, who escapes.
awliyāʾ, pl. of walī: allies, protectors.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ب ي ن

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّ اللَّهَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَلَمْ يَعْيَ بِخَلْقِهِنَّ بِقَادِرٍ عَلَىٰ أَن يُحْيِيَ الْمَوْتَىٰ ۚ بَلَىٰ إِنَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yaraw anna llāha lladhī khalaqa s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa wa-lam yaʿya bi-khalqihinna bi-qādirin ʿalā an yuḥyiya l-mawtā balā innahu ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīrun
versets.trad: Do they not see that God, who created the heavens and the earth and was not wearied by their creation, is able to give life to the dead? Yes indeed—He is powerful over all things!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī clarifies the letter of the verse: “do they not see” here means “do they not know”; and “was not wearied” means without being impotent or weakened. The One who created the heavens and the earth without the least toil all the more revives the dead: His power knows neither effort nor exhaustion.

mots.def: here raʾā in the sense of ʿalima: to know.

mots.racine: ع ي ي / خ ل ق

versets.ar: وَيَوْمَ يُعْرَضُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا عَلَى النَّارِ أَلَيْسَ هَٰذَا بِالْحَقِّ ۖ قَالُوا بَلَىٰ وَرَبِّنَا ۚ قَالَ فَذُوقُوا الْعَذَابَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yawma yuʿraḍu lladhīna kafarū ʿalā n-nāri a-laysa hāḏā bi-l-ḥaqqi qālū balā wa-rabbinā qāla fa-ḏūqū l-ʿaḏāba bimā kuntum takfurūna
versets.trad: On the Day when those who disbelieved are exposed to the Fire: “Is this not the truth?” They will say: “Yes, by our Lord!” He will say: “Then taste the chastisement for your disbelief!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī describes the scene as the ultimate sealing of the argument: it will be said to them, to clinch the proof: “Is this not the Real?” They will say: “Yes, by our Lord!” He will say: “Then taste the chastisement”—in requital for your denial. The confession of the Truth, wrung too late, no longer saves: it seals the justice of the decree.

mots.def: balā: yes (affirmative answer to a negation).

mots.racine: ذ و ق / ع ذ ب

versets.ar: فَاصْبِرْ كَمَا صَبَرَ أُولُو الْعَزْمِ مِنَ الرُّسُلِ وَلَا تَسْتَعْجِل لَّهُمْ ۚ كَأَنَّهُمْ يَوْمَ يَرَوْنَ مَا يُوعَدُونَ لَمْ يَلْبَثُوا إِلَّا سَاعَةً مِّن نَّهَارٍ ۚ بَلَاغٌ ۚ فَهَلْ يُهْلَكُ إِلَّا الْقَوْمُ الْفَاسِقُونَ
versets.tr: fa-ṣbir kamā ṣabara ulū l-ʿazmi mina r-rusuli wa-lā tastaʿjil lahum ka-annahum yawma yarawna mā yūʿadūna lam yalbaṯū illā sāʿatan min nahārin balāghun fa-hal yuhlaku illā l-qawmu l-fāsiqūna
versets.trad: So be patient, as the messengers of firm resolve were patient, and seek not to hasten it for them. On the Day they see what they are promised, it will be as though they had tarried but an hour of a day. A clear message! And shall any be destroyed but the transgressing people?
versets.commentaire: The “possessors of firm resolve” (ūlū al-ʿazm) are, according to Qushayrī, the prophets of striving, patience, and steadfastness—Noah, Abraham, Moses, Jesus, and Muḥammad are named. And he defines patience: to stand by the decree of God and to be firm in it, without complaint or constraint. As for the brevity of the sojourn, he extends it to the whole span of created beings: the duration of the world, from the beginning of its time to the end of its terms, is, set against the beginningless eternity, like a glance—or less still, for the azal has neither beginning nor end. What weight, then, has what occurred in an instant, be it good or ill?

mots.racine: ع ز م / ر س ل
س و ع / ن ه ر
mots.sens: but an hour of a day

mots.def: balāgh: communication, a message that suffices.
